---
title: "Diabetes Analysis"
author: "Ranjith Ambalakandy Kanakarajan"
date: "2026-03-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(smotefamily)
library(pROC)
```

```{r load-data}
diabetes <- read.csv("diabetes_012_health_indicators_BRFSS2015.csv")
```

```{r wrangle}
diabetes_bin <- diabetes %>%
  mutate(Diabetes_012 = factor(Diabetes_012,
                               levels = c(0, 1, 2),
                               labels = c("None", "Prediabetes", "Diabetes"))) %>%
  filter(Diabetes_012%in% c("None", "Diabetes")) %>%
  mutate(Diabetes_012 = droplevels(Diabetes_012))
```

```{r split}
set.seed(42)
idx        <- createDataPartition(diabetes_bin$Diabetes_012, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- diabetes_bin[idx, ]
test_data  <- diabetes_bin[-idx, ]
cat("Train:", nrow(train_data), "| Test:", nrow(test_data))
```

```{r smote}
train_numeric <- train_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.factor), as.numeric))

smote_result <- SMOTE(
  X        = train_numeric %>% select(-Diabetes_012),
  target   = train_numeric$Diabetes_012,
  K        = 5,
  dup_size = 0
)

train_balanced <- smote_result$data %>%
  rename(Diabetes_012 = class) %>%
  mutate(Diabetes_012 = factor(
    round(as.numeric(Diabetes_012)),
    levels = c(1, 2),
    labels = c("None", "Diabetes")
  ))

table(train_balanced$Diabetes_012)
```


```{r balanced-model}
# Retrain logistic regression on balanced data
balanced_model <- glm(Diabetes_012 ~ .,
                      data   = train_balanced,
                      family = binomial)

# Predict on the REAL test data
pred_prob2  <- predict(balanced_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
pred_class2 <- factor(ifelse(pred_prob2 > 0.5, "Diabetes", "None"),
                      levels = c("None", "Diabetes"))

# Evaluate
confusionMatrix(pred_class2, test_data$Diabetes_012, positive = "Diabetes")
```
```{r}
# After rf_model is trained:
varImpPlot(rf_model,
           main = "Random Forest — Variable Importance",
           col  = "steelblue")
```



```{r random-forest}
install.packages("randomForest")  # run in Console first if not installed
library(randomForest)

set.seed(42)
rf_model <- randomForest(Diabetes_012 ~ .,
                         data       = train_balanced,
                         ntree      = 100,
                         importance = TRUE)

# Evaluate on test data
rf_probs  <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, "Diabetes"]
rf_class  <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "class")

confusionMatrix(rf_class, test_data$Diabetes_012, positive = "Diabetes")
```

```{r random-forest-fixed}

library(randomForest)

# Ensure all categorical variables are proper factors in BOTH datasets
cols_to_factor <- c("HighBP","HighChol","CholCheck","Smoker","Stroke",
                    "HeartDiseaseorAttack","PhysActivity","Fruits","Veggies",
                    "HvyAlcoholConsump","AnyHealthcare","NoDocbcCost",
                    "DiffWalk","Sex")

train_rf <- train_balanced %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_factor), factor))

test_rf <- test_data %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_factor), factor))

# Train Random Forest on fixed data
set.seed(42)
rf_model2 <- randomForest(Diabetes_012 ~ .,
                          data       = train_rf,
                          ntree      = 100,
                          importance = TRUE)

# Evaluate
rf_class2 <- predict(rf_model2, newdata = test_rf, type = "class")
confusionMatrix(rf_class2, test_rf$Diabetes_012, positive = "Diabetes")
```

```{r}
# BMI is numeric (continuous) — should NOT be in the factor list
# Age is numeric (1-13 scale) — should NOT be in the factor list
# Only convert TRUE binary (0/1) variables

cols_to_factor <- c("HighBP","HighChol","CholCheck","Smoker","Stroke",
                    "HeartDiseaseorAttack","PhysActivity","Fruits","Veggies",
                    "HvyAlcoholConsump","AnyHealthcare","NoDocbcCost",
                    "DiffWalk","Sex")

train_rf <- train_balanced %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_factor), factor)) %>%
  mutate(BMI       = as.numeric(BMI),        # keep as number
         Age       = as.numeric(Age),        # keep as number
         GenHlth   = as.numeric(GenHlth),    # scale 1-5, keep numeric
         MentHlth  = as.numeric(MentHlth),   # scale 0-30, keep numeric
         PhysHlth  = as.numeric(PhysHlth),   # scale 0-30, keep numeric
         Education = as.numeric(Education),  # scale 1-6, keep numeric
         Income    = as.numeric(Income))     # scale 1-8, keep numeric

test_rf <- test_data %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_factor), factor)) %>%
  mutate(BMI       = as.numeric(BMI),
         Age       = as.numeric(Age),
         GenHlth   = as.numeric(GenHlth),
         MentHlth  = as.numeric(MentHlth),
         PhysHlth  = as.numeric(PhysHlth),
         Education = as.numeric(Education),
         Income    = as.numeric(Income))

# Confirm variable types look right
str(train_rf)

```



```{r}
# Step 1: Round all binary columns to 0 or 1 first, THEN factor
cols_to_factor <- c("HighBP","HighChol","CholCheck","Smoker","Stroke",
                    "HeartDiseaseorAttack","PhysActivity","Fruits","Veggies",
                    "HvyAlcoholConsump","AnyHealthcare","NoDocbcCost",
                    "DiffWalk","Sex")

train_rf <- train_balanced %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_factor), ~ factor(round(as.numeric(.))))) %>%
  mutate(BMI       = as.numeric(BMI),
         Age       = as.numeric(Age),
         GenHlth   = as.numeric(GenHlth),
         MentHlth  = as.numeric(MentHlth),
         PhysHlth  = as.numeric(PhysHlth),
         Education = as.numeric(Education),
         Income    = as.numeric(Income))

test_rf <- test_data %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_factor), ~ factor(round(as.numeric(.))))) %>%
  mutate(BMI       = as.numeric(BMI),
         Age       = as.numeric(Age),
         GenHlth   = as.numeric(GenHlth),
         MentHlth  = as.numeric(MentHlth),
         PhysHlth  = as.numeric(PhysHlth),
         Education = as.numeric(Education),
         Income    = as.numeric(Income))

# Confirm — HighBP should now show Factor w/ 2 levels "0","1"
str(train_rf)

```

```{r random-forest-fixed}
set.seed(42)
rf_model2 <- randomForest(Diabetes_012 ~ .,
                          data       = train_rf,
                          ntree      = 100,
                          importance = TRUE)

# Evaluate on test data
rf_class2 <- predict(rf_model2, newdata = test_rf, type = "class")
confusionMatrix(rf_class2, test_rf$Diabetes_012, positive = "Diabetes")
```
```{r variable-importance}

# Variable importance plot — answers "which risk factors matter most?"
varImpPlot(rf_model2,
           main = "Random Forest — Variable Importance for Diabetes Prediction",
           col  = "steelblue",
           pch  = 19)
```

```{r roc-curves}
library(pROC)

# Get probabilities from all 3 models
prob_log_imbal <- predict(full_model,     newdata = test_data,  type = "response")
prob_log_smote <- predict(balanced_model, newdata = test_data,  type = "response")
prob_rf        <- predict(rf_model2,      newdata = test_rf,    type = "prob")[,"Diabetes"]

# Build ROC objects
roc1 <- roc(test_data$Diabetes_012,  prob_log_imbal, levels = c("None","Diabetes"))
roc2 <- roc(test_data$Diabetes_012,  prob_log_smote, levels = c("None","Diabetes"))
roc3 <- roc(test_rf$Diabetes_012,    prob_rf,        levels = c("None","Diabetes"))

# Plot all three
plot(roc1, col="grey50", lwd=2, main="ROC Curve Comparison — All Models")
plot(roc2, col="blue",   lwd=2, add=TRUE)
plot(roc3, col="red",    lwd=2, add=TRUE)
abline(a=0, b=1, lty=2, col="black")  # random baseline

legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         paste("Logistic imbalanced  AUC =", round(auc(roc1), 3)),
         paste("Logistic SMOTE       AUC =", round(auc(roc2), 3)),
         paste("Random Forest        AUC =", round(auc(roc3), 3))
       ),
       col = c("grey50","blue","red"), lwd = 2)
```
```{r retrain-all-models}
# Quickly retrain logistic models (takes seconds)
full_model     <- glm(Diabetes_012 ~ ., data = train_data,     family = binomial)
balanced_model <- glm(Diabetes_012 ~ ., data = train_balanced, family = binomial)

# NOW run the ROC curves
library(pROC)

prob_log_imbal <- predict(full_model,     newdata = test_data, type = "response")
prob_log_smote <- predict(balanced_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
prob_rf        <- predict(rf_model2,      newdata = test_rf,   type = "prob")[,"Diabetes"]

roc1 <- roc(test_data$Diabetes_012, prob_log_imbal, levels = c("None","Diabetes"))
roc2 <- roc(test_data$Diabetes_012, prob_log_smote, levels = c("None","Diabetes"))
roc3 <- roc(test_rf$Diabetes_012,   prob_rf,        levels = c("None","Diabetes"))

plot(roc1, col="grey50", lwd=2, main="ROC Curve Comparison — All Models")
plot(roc2, col="blue",   lwd=2, add=TRUE)
plot(roc3, col="red",    lwd=2, add=TRUE)
abline(a=0, b=1, lty=2, col="black")

legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         paste("Logistic imbalanced AUC =", round(auc(roc1), 3)),
         paste("Logistic SMOTE      AUC =", round(auc(roc2), 3)),
         paste("Random Forest       AUC =", round(auc(roc3), 3))
       ),
       col = c("grey50","blue","red"), lwd = 2)
```

```


```
